title: The Precision Funnel — From Noise to Signal
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: Accumulated observations converge on truth through the precision funnel: the variance decreases as O(1/n), making the signal-to-noise ratio grow as O(√n)
content.proof: Central limit theorem: the mean of n independent observations has variance σ²/n. As n → ∞, the estimate converges to the true value. The 'funnel' is the shrinking confidence interval: at n observations, the 95% CI has width ≈ 3.92σ/√n. Each observation narrows the funnel. The precision feeling is the moment when the funnel becomes tight enough that the signal is undeniable.
content.benchmark: 100 observations: CI width σ/5. 10,000: CI width σ/50. 1M: CI width σ/500. Exponential signal emergence.
content.caveat: Assumes independent, identically distributed observations. Correlated observations converge slower. Systematic bias does NOT decrease with more observations — it shifts the target.
